rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: Muon: Newton-Schulz orthogonalizes momentum -> direct functional twin of orthogonal-direction-via-momentum step
MGUP: orthogonalization of momentum for curvature/conditioning adaptation -> same primitive
Coordinate momentum extension to Muon orthogonal-momentum family
Spectra: conditioning-aware suppression of dominant spectral components

fail_reason: 6 prior-art hits; Muon (rank 2, sem=0.86 func=0.89) and MGUP (rank 3, sem=0.83 func=0.85) are direct functional twins -- both apply Newton-Schulz orthogonalization to gradient momentum and step in the orthogonalized direction, which is the same primitive as SIFAKA-HOP's momentum-projected null-space step. The conditioning-trigger gating is a thin wrapper that does not rescue novelty. Spectra (rank 5) provides conditioning-triggered gradient modification. Independent scoring confirms prior agent's verdict.
